myths.statement: An NRI cannot vote in Indian elections.
myths.explanation: Fact: NRIs can register as overseas voters and vote in person at their constituency.

myths.statement: EVMs can be hacked via Bluetooth or Wi-Fi.
myths.explanation: Fact: Indian EVMs are standalone machines with no wireless communication capabilities.

myths.statement: Voting is mandatory by law in India.
myths.explanation: Fact: Voting is a right and a civic duty, but it is not legally mandatory.

myths.statement: If NOTA gets the most votes, a re-election is held.
myths.explanation: Fact: Currently, even if NOTA wins, the candidate with the next highest votes is declared the winner.

myths.statement: The Election Commission can disqualify a candidate for MCC violations.
myths.explanation: Fact: The ECI has the power to bar candidates from campaigning or even disqualify them in extreme cases.

myths.statement: If NOTA gets the most votes, the election is canceled.
myths.explanation: Fact: Currently in India, even if NOTA receives the highest number of votes, the candidate with the next highest votes is declared the winner.
